title: Cayratia verrucosa
intro: Cayratia verrucosa, also known as the rough bryony or hairy grape, is a perennial climber in the family Vitaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Cayratia verrucosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cayratia verrucosa is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various herbal preparations, including capsules and tinctures.
taxonomy: Cayratia verrucosa belongs to the family Vitaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Cayratia. The genus Cayratia is part of the Vitaceae family, which includes other plants such as grapes and kiwis. Cayratia verrucosa is a type of climbing vine native to tropical regions. It is often found in forests and along rocky outcrops in its native habitat.

It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses.
